description: Regression test for SCA-5589: pip ignorePipInstallErrors=true fallback must NOT report transitive dependencies as direct. The fix adds --no-deps to the per-line 'pip download', so when 'pip install -r requirements.txt' fails and the fallback fires, each requirements.txt entry is downloaded WITHOUT its transitive closure. Only neo4j-graphrag==1.6.0 (the sole resolvable entry) is reported; its transitives (pypdf, pydantic, etc.) are not downloaded by this path at all. Pre-fix the per-package download pulled the full closure and the flat collector reported all ~13 as direct. Discriminating assertion: expected_direct_dependencies_number == 1 (pre-fix was 13).

scan_output._comment_total_libs_scanned: Post-fix exactly 1 library (neo4j-graphrag) is scanned. Pre-fix: ~13 (the misclassified closure).

dependency_tree._comment_expected_total_dependencies_num: Post-fix total is 1: only neo4j-graphrag is downloaded (--no-deps prevents fetching its transitives in this fallback path). Pre-fix total was ~13.
dependency_tree._comment_expected_direct_dependencies_nu: CRITICAL / discriminating assertion for SCA-5589. MUST be exactly 1 (neo4j-graphrag only). Pre-fix: 13 (whole closure misclassified as direct). actual==13 means the bug is present or the fix is not deployed to this environment.
dependency_tree._comment_expected_transitive_dependencie: Post-fix: 0 transitives. --no-deps means pip does NOT download neo4j-graphrag's transitives in the ignorePipInstallErrors fallback, so nothing is nested under it (children: []). This is the intended trade-off of the fix (fewer deps in the degraded path, but correctly classified). NOTE: pre-fix was also 0 transitives (everything was a flat top-level direct), so this check alone does not distinguish fixed-from-broken; expected_direct_dependencies_number is the discriminating assertion.
dependency_tree._comment_direct_dependencies_names_list: STRICT name check: only neo4j-graphrag must be direct. 'eq' ensures NO other package (e.g. pypdf, pydantic) is at the top level. If any transitive appears here, the per-package download is still pulling the closure (bug present).

expected_log_lines._comment: Confirm the ignorePipInstallErrors flag is active - the trigger condition for the bug scenario.
Confirm pip install actually failed (bogus package triggered the failure). If absent, the fallback path was NOT triggered and the test is not validating the right scenario.
